test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_kitchen_remodel
test_cases.scenario: Complete renovation planning workflow for a standard kitchen remodel with typical inputs, moderate budget, and 6-month timeline constraint.

input.renovation_idea: Kitchen remodel with new cabinets, countertops, appliances, and flooring

test_cases.expected_behavior: All 10 phases execute successfully, producing a complete project plan with scope, contractor selection, budget, timeline, and documentation ready for execution.

test_cases.name: happy_path_bathroom_renovation
test_cases.scenario: Complete renovation planning for a single bathroom renovation with conservative contingency and tight timeline constraint.

input.renovation_idea: Bathroom renovation with new fixtures, tile, and vanity

test_cases.expected_behavior: All phases complete with conservative contingency allocation (20%) and timeline buffer to meet June 1 deadline. Historic home restrictions documented in scope.

test_cases.name: edge_case_minimal_scope_single_room
test_cases.scenario: Minimal renovation scope (single room paint and flooring) with very tight budget ($8,000) and aggressive contingency (10%).

input.renovation_idea: Paint and new flooring in bedroom

test_cases.expected_behavior: Scope definition correctly identifies simple project complexity. Contractor search focuses on small-job specialists. Budget breakdown shows minimal contingency (10%) with clear risk acknowledgment.

test_cases.name: edge_case_complex_multi_room_renovation
test_cases.scenario: Complex multi-room renovation (kitchen, 2 bathrooms, flooring throughout) with large budget ($150,000) and conservative contingency (20%).

input.renovation_idea: Complete kitchen remodel, 2 bathroom renovations, and new flooring throughout main level

property_details.known_issues: original plumbing and electrical, potential asbestos in flooring

test_cases.expected_behavior: Scope correctly identifies complex project. Site inspection identifies asbestos and plumbing/electrical issues. Conservative contingency (20%) allocated for unknowns. Critical path identified for multi-phase project. Stakeholder approval required.

test_cases.name: error_handling_scope_too_vague
test_cases.scenario: Renovation idea is too vague ('kitchen remodel') without specifics on work categories, triggering Phase 1 error handling for vague scope.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 1 quality gate fails due to vague scope. Error handling triggers Adjust response: request specificity on work categories (cabinets, countertops, appliances, flooring, etc.). Scope definition retried with more detailed input.

test_cases.name: error_handling_insufficient_qualified_contractors
test_cases.scenario: Contractor research finds only 2 qualified candidates (below 3-candidate minimum), triggering Phase 2 error handling.

input.renovation_idea: Specialized historic home restoration with period-accurate materials and techniques

property_details.known_issues: structural damage, original plaster, period fixtures

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 2 quality gate fails: only 2 qualified contractors found (minimum 3 required). Error handling triggers Adjust response: expand geographic search radius, relax experience criteria (accept contractors with less direct historic experience but strong references), or delay project. Contractor research retried.

test_cases.name: error_handling_bids_significantly_exceed_budget
test_cases.scenario: All contractor bids come in 35% above budget ceiling, triggering Phase 3/5 error handling for budget overrun.

input.renovation_idea: Kitchen remodel with high-end finishes and custom cabinetry

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 3 bid collection shows all bids 35% above $60,000 ceiling. Phase 5 budget breakdown triggers error handling: Adjust response uses scope reduction tree to identify cost-cutting options (remove premium finishes, reduce custom cabinetry, negotiate contractor pricing), or request stakeholder approval for budget increase.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_contractor_evaluation_red_flags
test_cases.scenario: Top-ranked contractor from evaluation has critical red flags from references (missed 3 deadlines, 25% budget overrun), triggering Phase 4 quality gate failure.

input.renovation_idea: Bathroom renovation with tile work and plumbing updates

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 4 contractor evaluation scores top candidate highest, but reference checks reveal 3 missed deadlines and 25% budget overrun (exceeds 20% threshold). Quality gate fails. Error handling triggers Adjust: move to next-ranked contractor and re-evaluate. If all candidates have red flags, loop back to Phase 2.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_scope_reduction_decision_tree
test_cases.scenario: Phase 1 scope definition creates decision tree with must-haves (cabinets, countertops, appliances: $35k) and nice-to-haves (backsplash, lighting, hardware: $15k) to handle budget constraints.

input.renovation_idea: Kitchen remodel with new cabinets, countertops, appliances, backsplash, lighting, and hardware

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 1 scope definition creates explicit decision tree: must-haves ($35k) vs. nice-to-haves ($15k). Quality gate passes because scope reduction tree clearly identifies which items can be cut if budget/timeline constraints exceeded. Tree enables Phase 5 cost-cutting decisions.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_site_inspection_major_issues
test_cases.scenario: Phase 0 site inspection discovers major structural issues (foundation cracks, outdated electrical) requiring scope revision and budget increase, testing inspection quality gate.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 0 inspection discovers foundation cracks and electrical code violations. Quality gate requires documentation of cost/timeline impacts. Scope revisions needed (true). Revised budget impact estimated at $25,000 additional. Loop back to Phase 1 to adjust scope and re-evaluate contractors.

Covered by standard workflows and red flags quality gate failure (tc_008)

Covered by standard workflows with varying timeline constraints
Covered by standard workflows with stakeholder approval
